description: baseline — Zig desktop calculator, 6 src files, raylib dependency, no network access, no auth/DB/API
64-byte display buffer has correct bounds checking; getDisplayText uses < 63 guard; formatResult uses fixedBufferStream
current_input has correct len >= 63 guard in appendDigit and len < 63 guard in appendDot
args_array at function scope, threads joined before return — already fixed, no vulnerability
Partials array written by separate threads without atomics; works on x86 but data race on ARM/weakly-ordered architectures
calculator.zig:278-282
column9: factorialParallel silently returns maxInt(u64) on overflow — no error signal to user, corrupts calculation result

description: Division by zero properly checked; error_msg set and 0 returned
sqrt/log/ln domain checks correct; error_msg set on invalid input
applyUnary checks NaN/Inf but compute() (binary ops) does not — chained operations propagate NaN from prior errors
If display alloc succeeds but current_input fails, display buffer is leaked before @panic
getDisplayText appends op_str to display buffer every call; at 60 FPS display grows "5 +  +  + ..." until buffer fills
Checks len > 0 before accessing len-1; no underflow possible
fixedBufferStream safely handles buffer size; overflow caught and ignored
Null terminator protects against stale data; std.mem.len stops at first \0
compute() returns Inf/NaN without checking; result stored in pending_value; subsequent operations use corrupted value
Only fires once per keypress; max 60 inputs/sec; buffer guards prevent overflow
Mouse coords compared against button bounds; raylib handles out-of-bounds gracefully
btn_w can go negative on extreme resize; raylib.drawRectangleRounded with negative width is UB in C library
Desktop app with no external asset loading; raylib attack surface not reachable in current usage
Cross-compile steps are no-ops; misleading but not exploitable
@panic("OOM") crashes app instead of graceful recovery; acceptable for calculator but not ideal
i64 conversion loses precision for floats > 2^53; @intFromFloat for values outside i64 range is UB
Standard deg-to-rad conversion; precision loss inherent to floating point, not exploitable
Spawn failure correctly falls back to sequential; no security impact
fixedBufferStream silently truncates long results; user sees incomplete output with no indication
64-byte stack buffer with ~16 chars of text; no overflow possible
All translations are compile-time constants; no user input flows into lookup
Theme colors are compile-time constants; no runtime modification possible
defer _ = gpa.deinit() discards leak detection result; memory leaks in debug mode go unreported
evaluate() clears pending_op even on compute error; failed operation state silently discarded
No upper bound on factorial input; factorialParallel(u64.max) causes infinite loop DoS; @intFromFloat UB for val > i64.max
